name: AXS Retest Hold Derisk v1
edge_mechanism: retest-hold continuation with explicit time-based derisking
thesis: AXS 1h is the structurally different lane for testing a retest-and-hold continuation idea that de-risks quickly if the expected expansion does not arrive. The edge should come from faster risk compression, not from assuming persistent trend follow-through.
invalidation_condition: Abort if retest-hold setups remain too sparse, if time-based derisking exits most trades before any edge appears, or if AXS noise overwhelms orderly retest structure.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A move retests fast value after a directional impulse, holds that retest, and either expands quickly or gets de-risked before stagnation turns into a full reversal.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In transitional small-cap tapes, some retests do continue, but many stall. A time-based derisk rule may preserve the continuation upside while reducing damage from failed persistence.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: AXS 1h is a useful stress-test lane because if the retest-hold logic is robust, it should still function on a noisier structurally different asset.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If AXS behaves as pure noise, the retest may hold briefly but not with enough structure for timed derisking to help.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the setup trades but de-risks too aggressively, relax the time-based reduction before touching the retest definition.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: More small scratches and partial wins, with expectancy depending on fast identification of the few real expansions.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: AXS 1h and at least one cleaner lane show credible density and improved downside control from time-based derisking.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, chaotic low-density output, or no measurable benefit from the derisk rule.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: moderate but controlled by broad retest logic

validation_basket.why_included: Stress-test lane for whether retest-hold continuation can survive on a noisy structurally different asset.
validation_basket.continue_if: Trades appear with credible density and time-based derisking improves damage control.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Noise destroys structure or derisking has no measurable value.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Frequent small scratches, some quick wins, and occasional meaningful continuations.

validation_basket.why_included: Determines whether the retest-and-derisk logic works on a cleaner lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH shows similar shape with better structure and less noise.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: Only AXS works, implying randomness rather than mechanism.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Cleaner retest holds and fewer scratch trades than AXS.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether time-based derisking has cross-asset validity on a second major lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC shows comparable benefit from fast derisking on held retests.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: The behavior does not generalize beyond one lane.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Fewer but cleaner retest-hold continuations than AXS.

long: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.12 * ATR_14 by selling 20%
Exit remaining 10% on close below EMA_55 or 5 bars
short: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.12 * ATR_14 by covering 20%
Exit remaining 10% on close above EMA_55 or 5 bars
